The '''Sith sniper rifle''' was a type of modified [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] used by the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]] against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. One such [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] [[Rifle/Legends|rifle]] was owned by<ref name="KOTOR" /> the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]]<ref name="Gender">Game files from ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' establish [[Kebla Yurt]] as having a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] head model.</ref> [[Kebla Yurt]] in [[3956 BBY]].<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' in [[3956 BBY]].</ref> She had the [[Weapon/Legends|weapon]] in stock at her [[Kebla Yurt's Equipment Emporium|Equipment Emporium]] in the [[Upper City]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. Unlike much of her other [[Weapon/Legends|weaponry]], the [[Sniper rifle/Legends|sniper rifle]] was not confiscated by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] when they [[Capture of Taris|occupied]] and quarantined the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]].<ref name="KOTOR" /> In the same [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="Date" /> Sith [[Sniper/Legends|sniper]] rifles were present at the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] on the Outer Rim planet [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] and the Sith-occupied [[Temple of the Ancients]],<ref name="KOTOR" /> located in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]<ref name="Campaign">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> on the [[Rakatan]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]].<ref name="KOTOR" />

The modified rifle functioned as an effective sniper rifle, costing 1,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Despite its potency, the rifle saw only sparse use by the Sith, who preferred more close-range combat.<ref name="KOTOR" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Sith sniper rifle first appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', developed by [[BioWare]]. It is found in containers the player is not required to open, and is also sold by Kebla Yurt, a non-player character whose interactions are optional and do not affect plot or alignment.<ref name="KOTOR" />
